resources.string: Rhythm Music Widget - Control Playback with responsive layouts supporting 2x1 to 5x5 grid sizes
Rhythm Music - Control Playback with 9 responsive layouts, Material 3 design, and real-time state updates
Changes will be applied to all active widgets on your home screen. Long-press the widget to resize it and see different layouts.
Apply blur effect to widget background
Automatically update widget when song changes
Rhythm supports multiple widget sizes from 2x1 to 5x5
Both legacy and modern Glance widgets are available
Tap the widget to open the app, buttons control playback
Long-press and resize for different layouts
Use app\'s custom notification style instead of system media notification
Get alerts for new versions and background update check results
Get background check results such as up-to-date and check errors
Show a notification when Rhythm Guard detects risky volume or exposure
Show notifications for scheduled breaks and active timeout countdowns
Receive friendly reminders and listening tips.
Use the player timeline for shuffle order.
Build a related queue when playback starts.
Choose which metadata the context queue should prioritize first.
Choose whether the context queue should stay available after playback ends.
Starting a track replaces the current queue.
Allow crossfade when repeating one track.
Apply crossfade when manually skipping songs.
Stop music when the app is closed.
Import from JSON, M3U, or PLS files
Create your first playlist to get started
Automatically create Recently Added and Most Played playlists
Progress and results for library scans and playlist actions
Scanning device storage for audio tracks…
Optimizing and saving audio track database…
Library scan completed: %1$d tracks, %2$d albums, %3$d artists indexed
Audio scanning finished, initializing background tasks…
Library scan failed. Check storage permissions and try again.
Library is optimized and ready: %1$d tracks, %2$d albums, %3$d artists
Export aborted: target playlist was not found.
Export aborted: no playlists found to backup.
The operation timed out. Please try again.
Playlist import failed. Please verify the file format.
✓ Ready to install. Tap to update.
🆕 A new version of Rhythm is here.
Countdown and status for sleep timer actions
Streaming service status and sync updates
🎵 %1$s is ready to play! Your library is syncing in the background.
Connection failed for %1$s. Tap to retry setup.
Session expired for %1$s. Tap to reconnect.
Sync failed. Check your connection and try again.
Quality switched to %1$s due to network changes
Network is slow. Stream quality reduced.
Streaming error on %1$s. Tap for details.
No internet. Switch to offline mode to continue listening.
This will permanently delete %1$d empty playlist%2$s. This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete \"%1$s\"? This action cannot be undone.
Current provider: %1$s\nSwitch to: %2$s? This will disconnect the currently active provider.
To apply changes to default playlists, we recommend restarting the app. You can continue using the app, but some playlists may not appear correctly until restart.
Hide specific songs and folders from your library
Only show songs from selected folders
Blacklist mode hides specific songs and folders from your library
Whitelist mode only shows songs from selected folders
Use folder management to organize your music collection
Enable scanning hidden and .nomedia audio paths during full media scans (strongest effect in whitelist folders).
Enter your Spotify API credentials to enable track search and Canvas videos.
You are currently using the app\'s default Spotify keys. For full functionality and to avoid rate limits, please input your own Client ID and Secret.

To get your Spotify API credentials:
• Go to developer.spotify.com
• Create a new app
• Copy the Client ID and Client Secret
A modern, feature-rich music player for Android
Built with modern Android technologies and Material Design 3
Special thanks to our amazing community members who have contributed to making Rhythm better through testing, feedback, suggestions, and collaboration.
Contribute to Rhythm through testing, feedback, or code contributions and join our amazing community!
Add some albums to your library to see featured content
Add some music to your library to see your artists
New albums will appear here as they\'re added to your library
Albums you add to your library will appear here
Late night vibes for the dreamers 🌙
Perfect time for your late night playlist 🎧
Let the night music play on 🌟
Start your day with the perfect soundtrack ✨
Let music energize your morning routine 🎵
Every morning is a new symphony waiting to begin 🌅
Coffee and music - the perfect morning blend ☕
Power through with your favorite beats 🚀
Let the rhythm guide your day 🎯
Turn up the focus with great tunes 🔥
Time to unwind with soothing melodies 🌅
Perfect time for your chill playlist 😌
Wind down with your favorite tunes 🌇
Perfect vibes for a peaceful night 🌟
Embrace the calm of the night 😴
Continue where you left off with \"%1$s\"
Ready to discover your next favorite song?
Listen to some music to get personalized recommendations
Start listening to see your recently played songs here
Add some music to see your artists here
Check storage permissions if files don\'t appear
Create your first playlist using the + button
To browse your music files and explore your device\'s storage, Rhythm needs permission to access your files and media.
This allows access only to audio files and folders containing music.
Adjust the speed and pitch of audio playback.
Your search history will appear here
Songs you play will appear here
Genre suggestions will appear here once detected
No genres detected in your library
Automatic update checking is disabled. You can still check manually.
App updates have been disabled in settings
Using efficient HTTP checks to detect updates while minimizing GitHub API calls. You\'ll get instant notifications when new versions are released.
Choose which update channel to follow:
Personalize every aspect of your listening experience with powerful features and complete privacy. Play your music offline, ad-free, with full control.
On newer Android versions, you\'ll see multiple permission requests. Please grant access to audio files and notifications for the best experience.
Keep your playlists, settings, and preferences safe with automatic backups. Restore everything when switching devices or after reinstalling - never lose your music collection.
Create backups or restore from existing backup files
You can manually create backups anytime in Settings > Backup & Restore
All settings, playlists, themes, and library customization
Restore from files or clipboard with one tap
Backups stored locally on your device for privacy
Customize your audio experience with haptic feedback, default screen, volume controls, queue behavior, shuffle settings, lyrics display, and equalizer settings.
API: Online lyrics • Embedded: Metadata lyrics • Local: .lrc files
Equalizer: Fine-tune audio frequencies in Settings > Equalizer
Sleep Timer: Auto-stop playback after set time (access from Now Playing screen)
Customize how your albums, artists, and songs are displayed. Choose your preferred layout, sorting order, and tab arrangement.
Display albums in grid layout for better visual browsing
Display artists in grid layout with profile pictures
Display synchronized lyrics in the player screen
Customize tab order in Library for quick access to your favorites
Create and manage playlists directly from the Library screen
Advanced display and sorting options available in Settings > Library Settings
Default playlists (Recently Added, Most Played) can be toggled in Settings > Playlists
Personalize Rhythm\'s appearance to match your style. Choose your system theme, font, or Material You dynamic colors (Android 12+).
Custom color schemes, album art colors, and fonts
Custom color schemes and album art color extraction
Advanced theme options available in Settings > Theme Customization
Set up Rhythm Guard to keep your listening safer with age-aware defaults and manual protection controls.
Age profile helps Rhythm Guard recommend safer listening limits.
Auto mode applies safer defaults with minimal setup.
You can tune alerts and break reminders later in Settings.
Manual mode gives you full control over warnings and thresholds.
Core setup is done. Continue for advanced customization, or finish now and explore the rest later from Settings.
Full tour includes playback, theme, gesture, and media scan options.
You can reopen Rhythm Tour anytime from Settings > Developer & Debugging.
Nothing here is permanent. All choices can be changed later.
Custom color schemes, album art colors, and fonts
Control which audio files appear in your library. Use Blacklist mode to hide unwanted files (ringtones, notifications), or Whitelist mode to only show specific music folders.
Blacklist Mode: Hide specific songs and folders (ringtones, notifications)
Whitelist Mode: Show only songs from selected folders
Configure scanning in Settings > Media Scan
Manage cache size and storage in Settings > Cache Management
Rhythm is ready to play! Your music library is being scanned in the background. Here\'s what\'s configured:
Browse your songs, albums, and artists
All settings can be changed anytime in Settings
All necessary permissions have been granted. You are ready to proceed!
To grant the required permissions, please navigate to the app settings on your device.
Rhythm requires certain permissions to function correctly. Please grant them to continue.
To fully utilize Rhythm\'s features, we need your permission to access certain device capabilities.
Read your local music files and display them in the app
Connect to Bluetooth speakers, headphones, and audio devices
Show playback controls and music information in notifications
Grant all permissions for the best experience
You can change permissions later in Settings
Rhythm respects your privacy and data
Enable subtle vibrations on interactions for a more tactile experience.
Choose which screen opens when you launch Rhythm
Allow your device\'s physical volume buttons to control Rhythm\'s playback volume.
Automatically create a contextual queue from the album/artist when playing a song.
Replace the entire queue when playing a new song instead of adding to it.
Keep your repeat mode setting across app restarts.
Keep your shuffle on/off state across app restarts.
Let the media player handle shuffle (recommended: OFF for manual shuffle).
Display synchronized lyrics when available for your favorite songs.
Choose which lyrics source to try first (with automatic fallback to others)
Reorder Songs, Playlists, Albums, Artists, and Explorer tabs
Automatically switch between light and dark themes based on your device\'s system settings.
Manually enable or disable dark mode for the application.
Apply colors extracted from your device\'s wallpaper for a cohesive look.
Personalize your playback interfaces. Toggle between the clean, modern Rhythm design or the vibrant, fluid Expressive design.
Get the latest features, improvements, and bug fixes automatically from GitHub.
Allow the app to check for and download updates
Check for updates from Rhythm\'s GitHub repo automatically
Get notified when new versions are available
Use efficient checks to save GitHub API calls
How often to check for updates
Your music library organization preferences are set
Your custom theme and appearance settings are ready
Control how Rhythm displays music playback in your notification shade. Choose between custom styled notifications or the standard system media controls.
Use Rhythm\'s enhanced notification design with additional controls and styling
See current track info at a glance
Navigate your music library faster with intuitive swipe and tap gestures. Customize gestures for the mini player and full player screens.
Swipe left/right on mini player to skip tracks
Swipe down on full player to return to mini player
Swipe on artwork to skip to next/previous song
Double tap album art to like the current song
Swipe gestures work in both directions
Quick swipes respond faster than slow ones
Add Rhythm widgets to your launcher for quick music control. Customize what information is displayed and how the widget looks.
Display album artwork in the widget
Display the current artist in the widget
Display the album title in the widget
Update widget automatically when song changes
Tap the widget to open the app
Resize widgets for different layouts (2x1 to 5x5)
Widgets update in real-time during playback
Enhance your listening experience by connecting to external services. Get album art, lyrics, scrobble your plays, and share what you\'re listening to.
Fetch high-quality album artwork and metadata
Download synchronized lyrics for your songs
Access metadata and recommendations from YouTube Music
Fetch album art and metadata from Spotify
Track your listening history on Last.fm
Show what you\'re listening to on Discord
Share playback status with other apps
These features send limited data (song titles, artists) to external services. Your personal data and music files stay on your device. You can enable or disable these at any time in Settings.
Discover insights about your music habits. Track your listening time, most played songs, favorite artists, and more with beautiful visualizations.
Display a listening stats widget on your home screen
Rate your songs to track favorites and build smart playlists
Track total listening time by day, week, or month
Stats are calculated locally on your device based on your playback history. No data is sent to external servers. Access detailed stats anytime from the home screen or settings.
Customize your theme with colors, fonts, and festive effects
Enable haptic feedback for tactile responses
Control playback faster with intuitive swipes and taps, no extra menus needed.
Swipe up on mini player to open full player, then swipe down to dismiss when done.
Swipe artwork to switch tracks and double tap album art for quick play or pause.
Use media scan to hide unwanted files from your library
Protect your hearing by enabling Rhythm Guard
Rhythm Guard automatically manages safe listening limits
You are manually controlling Rhythm Guard limits
Quick access to your music collection
Set the maximum size for cached data
Automatically clear cached data when app closes
Choose where to look for Lyrics
Embedded lyrics are extracted from ID3v2 USLT tags in your audio files. For best results, use properly tagged MP3/FLAC files.
Prevent screen from turning off while lyrics are visible
Display blurred album artwork behind full-screen lyrics
Album Art colors adapt dynamically to your music
Material You uses system wallpaper colors (Android 12+)
Import custom fonts to personalize typography
Choose where to load fonts from
Safeguard your personalized settings, playlists, and preferences. Create backups manually or enable automatic weekly backups for peace of mind.
Choose where to save your backup file
All settings, playlists, themes, and library customization
Restore from files or clipboard with one tap
Backups stored locally on your device for privacy
Blocked songs and folders won\'t appear in search results, recommendations, or auto-play queues
All your songs are available for playback
Tap the button above to add folders to the blacklist
Add songs from your library to start listening
Add more songs to keep the music playing
You have %1$d song%2$s in the current queue.
How would you like to play \"%1$s\"?
Built with amazing open source projects
This app is licensed under GPL-3.0. Libraries are licensed under Apache 2.0 or compatible licenses
Player chip order and visibility saved
Configure sources, behaviors, layout, size, and styling of lyrics
Sync adjustment only works with time-synced lyrics (LRC format). Use Reset to fetch synced lyrics if available.

Enter lyrics here…

You can use LRC format:
[00:12.00] Line one
[00:17.00] Line two
Rhythm needs permission to modify this audio file\'s metadata. You may see a system dialog asking for access - please allow it to save your changes.
Create your first playlist to add songs
Manage cached data including images, temporary files, and other app data.
You are currently using the beta release of Rhythm. This version is still in development and may contain bugs, incomplete features, or unexpected behavior.
Enable the equalizer to customize your audio experience.
Color schemes are only available when using Custom color source.
Switch to Custom in the Color Source setting to access predefined schemes.
Custom colors are only available when using Custom color source.
Switch to Custom in the Color Source setting to create your own colors.
System fonts are only available when using System font source.
Switch to System in the Font Source setting to choose from available fonts.
All settings, playlists, themes, and library customization
Restore from files or clipboard with one tap
Backups stored locally on your device for privacy
Reorder tabs to customize your library experience
Control which external API services are active to enhance your music experience. Toggle services on/off to manage data usage and functionality.
Choose where Rhythm should look for lyrics first
Select the default playlist tap action
Show an action chooser each time
Load playlist and start from selected track
Default behavior when queue already has tracks
Select the default queue conflict action
Show an action chooser each time
Clear queue and play selected song
Add song at the end of queue
Always add selected track to queue
App updates have been disabled in settings
Automatic update checking is disabled. You can still check manually.
How often should Rhythm check for updates?
More experimental features coming soon in future updates
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Send playback info to Last.fm, Pano Scrobbler, and compatible apps
This allows you to track your listening history, discover new music, and share your music taste with friends.

• Last.fm for Android
• Pano Scrobbler
• Simple Last.fm Scrobbler
• Other SLS API compatible apps
1. Install a scrobbling app (e.g., Pano Scrobbler from F-Droid)\n2. Enable scrobbling here\n3. Your listening activity will be automatically tracked!
Show what you\'re listening to in your Discord status
Shows song title, artist, album, and playback progress.
• Discord for Android\n• Discord must be running\n• Music status must be enabled in Discord settings

1. Install Discord for Android
2. Enable this setting in Rhythm
3. Open Discord and enable "Display current activity" in User Settings → Activity Status
4. Your now playing info will appear in your Discord status!
Only broadcasts when music is playing. You can toggle this on/off anytime. Discord controls who can see your activity status.
Send playback info to Tasker, KWGT, and automation apps
Send synced lyric line as Bluetooth track title and move Song - Artist into artist metadata

Status broadcasting sends standard Android music player intents that automation and widget apps listen for. This allows third-party apps to display your now playing info or trigger automations based on what you\'re listening to.

• Tasker (automation)
• KWGT/KLWP (Kustom widgets)
• MacroDroid
• AutoApps
• Zooper Widget
• Any app that listens to music player intents

1. Install your automation/widget app (e.g., Tasker, KWGT)
2. Enable this setting
3. Configure your app to listen for:
 • com.android.music.metachanged (track changes)
 • com.android.music.playstatechanged (play/pause)
4. Access song data via standard intents!

• Display now playing in custom widgets
• Trigger smart home scenes based on music
• Auto-pause music when certain apps open
• Log your listening history
• Control other apps based on playback state
Use embedded replay gain tags for volume
Sleep timer controls will be implemented here
What happens when the timer ends
Set hours and minutes for the sleep timer
Synchronized lyrics not available for this format.
Include default playlists (Favorites, Recently Added, Most Played)
Select a playlist file to import:
This may take a few moments…
Pause playback when volume reaches zero
Keep collaborations grouped under album artists
Choose a setting from the list to configure
All songs are already in this playlist.
Are you sure you want to delete \'%1$s\'? This action cannot be undone.
Choose which release family Rhythm should use for OTA updates.
Always download the GitHub release assets.
Always download the F-Droid release assets.

Successfully imported %1$d playlists.
%2$s

Restart the app to apply changes.
Failed to embed lyrics in file
Set hours and minutes for the sleep timer
Connect to Bluetooth, Cast, or other devices
Use the output switcher to cast your music to Bluetooth devices, Chromecast, or other compatible devices. Android\'s native media routing handles seamless playback transfer.
Stream music to Chromecast, smart TVs, or speakers
Tap to disconnect from this device

Your complete Rhythm backup has been created including:

• All playlists and their songs
• Favorites and play history
• App settings and preferences
• Equalizer configurations
• Theme customizations

Your Rhythm data has been restored successfully including:

• All playlists and their songs
• Favorites and play history
• App settings and preferences
• Equalizer configurations
• Theme customizations
Configure your audio devices with optimized AutoEQ profiles for the best listening experience.
Add your headphones or speakers to get personalized EQ settings
Paste EQ settings from autoeq.app or other sources.

Supported formats:
• FixedBandEQ text (AutoEQ)
• JSON format
• CSV (10 band values)
Share your EQ settings with others or save for backup.
No active EQ settings to export. Please enable the equalizer and configure it first.
Are you sure you want to delete \'%1$s\'? This action cannot be undone.
Status results for background update checks
Friendly listening reminders and tips from Rhythm
Rhythm Guard break and timeout reminders
Rhythm is preparing the media session.
Rhythm is connecting the media controller.
Rhythm could not start. Please reopen the app.
Control gradient and blur behind album sheets
Show Song Art Instead of Album Art
Prefer embedded artwork from each audio file (formerly Ignore MediaStore Covers)
Show full-resolution embedded song artwork without compression
Artwork mode changed. Restart the app to clear caches and run a full media rescan with updated artwork sources.
Embedded artwork cache currently uses %1$s across %2$d files
Prune old artwork entries using cache limits
Listening time, top tracks and trends
Listening health guardrails powered by Rhythm Stats
Listening health guardrails powered by Rhythm Stats
Use your Rhythm Stats activity to keep listening habits safer with age-aware recommendations and manual warnings.
Use your Rhythm Stats activity to keep listening habits safer with age-aware recommendations and manual warnings.
Choose how Rhythm Guard manages listening health
Choose how Rhythm Guard manages listening health
Recommended safe volume up to %1$d%% and around %2$d minutes per day.
Recommended safe volume up to %1$d%% and around %2$d minutes per day.
Live hearing safety snapshot based on real-time system volume and today\'s listening load.
Live hearing safety snapshot based on real-time system volume and today\'s listening load.
Rhythm Guard automatically keeps safer playback defaults based on your age profile.
Rhythm Guard automatically keeps safer playback defaults based on your age profile.
System volume: %1$s, Pause at zero: %2$s, Normalization: %3$s, ReplayGain: %4$s
System volume: %1$s, Pause at zero: %2$s, Normalization: %3$s, ReplayGain: %4$s
Warn when live volume or manual toggles may be risky
Warn when live volume or manual toggles may be risky
Set when Rhythm Guard should warn about potentially harmful volume.
Set when Rhythm Guard should warn about potentially harmful volume.
Apply Volume Limits On Phone Speaker
Apply Volume Limits On Phone Speaker
When off, Rhythm Guard skips volume clamping and volume warnings on built-in speaker output.
When off, Rhythm Guard skips volume clamping and volume warnings on built-in speaker output.
Fine-tune exposure alerts, reminder timeout, and default break resume interval.
Auto mode manages listening thresholds and intervals for you. Current policy: %1$s daily target, %2$s warning cooldown, %3$s default timeout.
Current volume is above your warning threshold (%1$d%%). Consider reducing volume.
Current volume is above your warning threshold (%1$d%%). Consider reducing volume.
You reached %1$s today, above the suggested %2$s. Consider taking a listening break.
You reached %1$s today, above the suggested %2$s. Consider taking a listening break.
Turning off audio normalization may increase loudness swings.
Turning off audio normalization may increase loudness swings.
Turning off ReplayGain can raise peak volume unexpectedly.
Turning off ReplayGain can raise peak volume unexpectedly.
Disabling pause-on-zero removes a protective safeguard.
Disabling pause-on-zero removes a protective safeguard.
System media volume is %1$d%% in real time, above your current protection limit of %2$d%%. Lower volume to reduce hearing risk.
System media volume is %1$d%% in real time, above your current protection limit of %2$d%%. Lower volume to reduce hearing risk.
Estimated listening today is %1$d minutes, above your recommended %2$d minutes. Consider a short break and lower volume for the next session.
Estimated listening today is %1$d minutes, above your recommended %2$d minutes. Consider a short break and lower volume for the next session.
Keep listening at or below your threshold.
Lower volume soon and avoid long sessions.
Reduce volume now and take a short break.
Pause playback now and resume at a safer level.
You\'ve listened for %1$s today (recommended %2$s). Take a short break to protect your hearing.
You\'ve listened for %1$s today (recommended %2$s). Take a short break to protect your hearing.
Listening break triggered: today %1$s exceeds recommended %2$s.
Quickly apply tested guardrail bundles for your listening style.
Plot twist: your ears started a union and called for a snack break. Rhythm Guard paused playback so everyone can cool down.
Plot twist: your ears started a union and called for a snack break. Rhythm Guard paused playback so everyone can cool down.
No automatic adjustments or warnings are applied. Playback behavior stays unchanged.
No automatic adjustments or warnings are applied. Playback behavior stays unchanged.
Set how much daily listening triggers a safety alert
Set the cooldown between repeated Rhythm Guard alerts
Set cooldown after a timeout ends before another exposure timeout can trigger
Apply Gentle, Balanced, or Strict guardrail bundles in one tap
Set how long scheduled listening breaks last by default
Enable or disable Rhythm Guard protections
Start playing music to see your stats
Album covers, artist photos, and thumbnails
Cache helps load content faster and reduces data usage
Set a cache limit to manage storage automatically
Clearing cache will not delete your music or playlists
Let the media player handle shuffle
Automatically add related songs when playing
Playing a song directly clears the current queue.
Save repeat mode between app restarts
Save shuffle state between app restarts
Swipe down on player to close it
Swipe left/right on artwork to skip tracks
Double tap on artwork to play/pause
Replace queue and play from selected song
Automatically add songs to queue without asking
Songs with multiple artists are split using separators
Each artist appears separately in your library
Use custom separators for better organization
Ask what to do when queue is not empty
Show collaboration albums under main artist

When enabled, Rhythm will automatically split artist tags containing multiple artists. This is useful for songs downloaded with yt-dlp or other tools that use delimiters like \'/\' to separate artists.\n\nBackslash (\\\\) can be used to escape delimiters.
Tap to switch between app and system volume
Control system volume instead of app volume

When enabled, Rhythm will automatically split artist tags containing multiple artists. This is useful for songs downloaded with yt-dlp or other tools that use delimiters like \'/\' to separate artists.\\n\\nBackslash (\\\\) can be used to escape delimiters.
A modern, feature-rich music player for Android
At least one section must be visible
At least one tab must be visible
Tab order and visibility reset to default
At least one chip must be visible
Reset to default order and visibility
Configure your audio devices with optimized AutoEQ profiles for the best listening experience.
Add your headphones or speakers to get personalized EQ settings
Are you sure you want to delete \'%1$s\'? This action cannot be undone.
Paste EQ settings from autoeq.app or other sources.
Supported formats:\\n\u2022 FixedBandEQ text (AutoEQ)\\n\u2022 JSON format\\n\u2022 CSV (10 band values)
Invalid backup format or corrupted data
Unable to read the backup file
No backup data found in clipboard. Please copy a backup first.
Automatic headphone equalization from frequency responses by Jaakko Pasanen
Android\'s modern toolkit for building native UI
Modern media playback library for Android
Image loading library for Android backed by Kotlin Coroutines
Type-safe HTTP client for Android and Java
Adjust the amount of festive decorations shown
Adjusts the roundness of album artwork corners
Choose what to show in header
Customize the compact player that appears at the bottom of your screen.
Toggle individual decoration elements by position
Google Material Design Team for design principles
Double tap artwork for quick play/pause
Enable lyrics to see synchronized lyrics while playing
No crash logs found. Good job!
Personalize your player experience with custom chip layouts, visual effects, and display preferences.
Sleep timer controls will be implemented here
Start all screens with collapsed header
Statistics update based on listening habits
Swipe down on mini player or full player to dismiss
Swipe up on mini player to open full player
Tap \'Chip Order & Visibility\' to customize action chips
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Toggle widgets to customize your home screen
A modern, feature-rich music player for Android
Adjust snowflake size (smaller size = more snowflakes)
Adjust the amount of festive decorations shown
Adjusts the roundness of album artwork corners
Canvas backgrounds add animated visuals to supported songs
Choose what to show in header
Customize the compact player that appears at the bottom of your screen.
Disable unused gestures for faster response
Disable unused sections for faster loading
More festivals will be added in future updates
Toggle individual decoration elements by position

When enabled, Rhythm will automatically split artist tags containing multiple artists. This is useful for songs downloaded with yt-dlp or other tools that use delimiters like \'/\' to separate artists.\n\nBackslash (\\) can be used to escape delimiters.
• Embedded lyrics are stored in your audio files\n
• Google Material Design Team for design principles\n
Ask before replacing or extending queue for single-track play.
Always add single-track play to the queue.
Disabled while Replace Queue on Play is on.
Restore queue and position after restart.
Choose how list actions modify an active queue
Clear current queue and start this list immediately.
Show a popup whenever a list action conflicts with the current queue.
Insert the list after the current song and start from your selected track.
Append the list to the end of queue and start from your selected track.
Current queue has %1$d tracks. Incoming list has %2$d tracks.
Start this list as the active queue now.
Place this list after current song and begin playback from it.
Append this list to queue end and start playback from it.
How audio is routed when a DAC is connected. App mode requests exclusive USB bit-perfect routing on supported devices.
Rhythm handles audio directly with high-resolution mode and requests exclusive USB bit-perfect routing on supported devices. Restart required.
Uses Android system audio routing. Audio may be resampled to 48 kHz by the OS mixer.
Android decides how to route audio. Resampling depends on system settings.
How your library data is stored locally
Uses Room database for structured storage. Better for large libraries. Restart required.
Uses JSON file for simple, portable storage. Default option.
Save current library cache to a shareable JSON file
Load song data from a JSON backup file
Re-sync Room database from current library data
View Room database song count and size
View JSON cache file size and song count
Automatically clear cache when exiting app
Cache was cleared. Restart the app to refresh everything cleanly.
Remove all cached data, including embedded artwork cache entries
Cache includes album art, temporary files, and app data
Clearing cache may temporarily slow down the app
Cached data will rebuild automatically as needed
Allow the app to check for and download updates
Automatically check for updates from Rhythm\'s GitHub repo
Get notified when new versions are available
Use efficient checks to save GitHub API calls
Beta - Early access to new features
Connect at least one service to enter Rhythm Go streaming navigation.
Choose which connected service Rhythm Go should prioritize by default.
Select your preferred audio quality for streaming playback.
Data saver mode with reduced quality (~96 kbps).
Balanced quality and data usage (~160 kbps).
Open app-wide settings shared across modes
Manage the selected service below to connect your account.
Connect your account to start streaming in Rhythm Go.
Required for providers that need a server address.
Store credentials locally so Rhythm can reconnect automatically later.
Fill in the required fields to connect this service.
Connect %1$s to load Home and Search.
This may take a moment after you connect or refresh.
Try refreshing after your service finishes syncing.
Find a song, artist, or album
No results from %1$s yet. Integration loading is in progress.
Connect Navidrome and compatible Subsonic servers
Stream from your personal Jellyfin library
Sign in and stream your cloud music collection
Sign in and stream your QQ Music content
Log audio codec and format info for debugging. View in Logcat with tag \'CodecMonitor\'

Your complete Rhythm backup has been created including:

• All app settings and preferences
• Library configuration
• Theme customization

The backup has been saved and also copied to your clipboard for easy sharing.

Your Rhythm data has been restored successfully including:

• All app settings and preferences
• Library configuration
• Theme customization

Please restart the app for all changes to take full effect.
Swipe up/down to open/dismiss, left/right to skip tracks
Close player by swiping down on the screen
Swipe left/right on album artwork to skip tracks
Double tap on album art to play/pause
Swipe up on mini player to open full player
Swipe down on mini player or full player to dismiss
Double tap artwork for quick play/pause
Disable unused gestures for faster response

Notify when listening risk warnings are triggered
Notify for scheduled breaks and timeout countdowns

Pure black theme for OLED displays
Show festive decorations across the app
Uh oh! Looks like Rhythm hit a sour note!
Don\'t fret! Our app just had a little \'oopsie\'. Please share the crash details below – it\'s like a secret message for our developers to fix things! Then, let\'s try that again, shall we?
Enable at least one field to edit
Please enter a value for at least one enabled field
Enable fields you want to update. Disabled fields stay unchanged.
Supported formats: • FixedBandEQ text (AutoEQ) • JSON format • CSV (10 band values)
Find a profile for this device...
Section order and visibility reset to default
Home section order and visibility saved
Permission denied. Changes saved to library only.
Permission required to modify file metadata
Tap songs to select multiple, then add them all at once
Tap the remove action to update folder access
Enable festive decorations and seasonal themes
Select how you\'d like to experience your music. You can change this anytime in settings.
Play audio files stored directly on your device. Supports advanced local directory scanning, statistics, and theme matching.
Connect to Jellyfin, Subsonic, or Navidrome servers to stream your entire library on the go, with caching support for offline listening.
Switch easily anytime in Settings > Integration.
Local mode scans and catalogs audio files on your device.
GO Streaming connects via subsonic/jellyfin API server.
Connect your Subsonic/Navidrome or Jellyfin server to start streaming.
Supports Navidrome, gonic, and all subsonic APIs.
Enable caching in Settings for offline playback later.
Connection test ensures server credentials are correct.
Remember to include http:// or https://
Failed to add to queue: Player not initialized
Failed to play next: Player not initialized

Streaming section order and visibility saved
Create playlists on your server to see them here.
Failed to play next: Unable to resolve URL
Failed to add to queue: Unable to resolve URL

Thanks to everyone supporting Rhythm\'s development.
Select what to include in this backup file.
Select which sections from the backup should be restored.

Material 3 Expressive introduces organic, playful shapes like flowers, hearts, cookies, and more. These shapes create a unique, expressive experience that makes your music app feel more personal and fun. Enable expressive shapes to bring your music collection to life with beautiful, age-appropriate organic forms.
Select a theme for all components
Festive decorations add a touch of celebration to your music experience. When auto-detect is enabled, decorations appear automatically during holidays like Christmas (Dec 15-31).
Immersive mode is active. Only supported discover controls are shown.
Choose which online lyrics API to search first
• Embedded lyrics are stored in your audio files
Choose between Rhythm Default or Expressive theme
Integrated directly into the MiniPlayer background
Choose a setting from the left panel
Tap on any setting option to view and modify its preferences here
Refer to release notes on GitHub releases page.
Rhythm uses Material 3 Expressive design with dynamic colors
Widget adapts from 1x1 play button to 5x5+ full layout
Long-press and resize for different expressive layouts
Playback was paused because the volume reached zero. Raise the volume to resume listening.
Are you sure you want to delete \'%1$s\'? This action cannot be undone.

Successfully imported %1$d playlists.

Restart the app to apply changes.
Are you sure you want to remove %1$d songs from this playlist?
Display favorite toggle on large widgets
Adapts dynamically to device wallpaper palette and system accent tones
Deep flat charcoal backdrop, optimal for premium OLED dark screen styling
Sleek translucent glass floating elegantly over home screen wallpapers
Signature deep violet backdrop with signature accent tints and highlights
Show contextual suggestions at the top
Optimize haptics, decoding, marquee, and artwork fetching to reduce lag
Refresh your music player with lively AMOLED black and beautiful pastel colors.
Make Rhythm truly yours by matching the app\'s accent to your system.
Avoid unwanted audio clips. Explicitly define which folders we should scan for music.
Keep voice notes and ringtones away from your playlists using focus folders.
Drift off to sleep while your music is playing. We\'ll automatically pause it.
Set an automated sleep timer before bed so playback stops perfectly.
Elevate your experience. Boost the bass and adjust frequencies using the Equalizer.
Tune the sound to your headphones securely with our advanced audio effects.
Auto backup is active. Verify your backup sections so restores stay clean.
Protection enabled: schedule looks good. Run a manual backup before big edits.
Auto backup is off. Enable it to protect playlists and Rhythm Guard stats.
No safety net yet. Turn on backups to avoid losing your setup.
Updates are enabled. Keep them on for fixes and performance improvements.
You are set to receive updates. Check channel preferences for stability vs freshness.
Updates are disabled. Re-enable them to stay protected and current.
You are pinned to the current build. Turn updates back on when ready.
Player swipe-to-skip is active. Tune queue rules for smoother transitions.
Gesture skip is on. Match queue behavior to your listening flow.
Swipe-to-skip is off. Queue controls can help keep playback efficient.
Prefer explicit controls? Fine-tune queue behavior for predictable playback.
Seek buttons are visible. Customize player controls to reduce clutter.
Control-rich player enabled. Tweak layout for your thumb reach.
Minimal player controls enabled. Add seek buttons if you miss precise jumps.
Clean layout active. Re-enable seek controls for finer playback handling.
Mini player progress is visible. Choose a style that matches your theme.
Progress bar is active on mini player. Try shape and corner tweaks next.
Mini player progress is hidden. Enable it for quick track awareness.
Compact mode is minimal now. Turn progress on for better glanceability.
Ratings are enabled. Use library settings to sharpen discovery and sorting.
Your library supports ratings. Tune scan and grouping for cleaner browsing.
Ratings are disabled. Library settings can still improve structure and speed.
Simplified library mode detected. Adjust grouping for faster navigation.
Try audio file metadata → Online APIs → .lrc files
Try online services → Audio metadata → .lrc files
Try .lrc files → Audio metadata → Online APIs
Font source changed. Restart the app to apply the new font.

Use Material You colors from your wallpaper
Import and use a custom font file
Modern, clean sans-serif font from Google
Elegant serif font with a classic, traditional appearance
Clean and modern sans-serif font, highly readable
Monospace font perfect for technical content
Rounded font with a softer, friendlier appearance
Material 3 baseline purple with rosy pink accents
Cozy orange and red tones for a warm atmosphere
Refreshing blue and teal tones for a calming vibe
Natural green tones inspired by nature
Minimalist grayscale for a clean, modern look
Calming purple and lavender tones for relaxation
Deep blues and aquamarines for oceanic serenity
Vibrant greens and blues inspired by the aurora borealis
Rich amber and gold tones for a luxurious feel
Bold burgundy and crimson shades for drama
Fresh emerald greens with natural forest hues
Fresh and clean cyan and mint green tones
Open a full-screen lyrics screen by tapping the lyrics view
Automatically hide controls in full-screen lyrics screen after a few seconds of inactivity
Prefer lyrics embedded in audio files, fallback to online APIs
Prefer online APIs (Rhythm word-by-word, LRCLib), fallback to embedded
Prefer local .lrc files, then embedded lyrics, then online APIs
Attempt fallback APIs if the preferred API fails to return lyrics
Make the text of active lyrics bold
Trim leading and trailing whitespace from lyric lines
Disable transitions and animations in the lyrics view
Try to automatically sync syllable or word translations

Additional Sync Details:
• Online APIs provide high-quality synced lyrics
• Rhythm offers word-by-word sync
• LRCLib provides free line-by-line sync
Clear headphone profile, use standard EQ presets
Could not parse EQ settings. Check the format.
This song will no longer be hidden from your library.
This song will be hidden from your library and excluded from playback.
This action can be undone anytime.
All songs in this folder will be visible again.
All songs in this folder will be hidden and excluded from playback.

How would you like to play \"%1$s\"?
Replace queue and play this song
Keep current queue and add song
To ensure all imported playlists work correctly, we recommend restarting the app. You can continue using the app normally, but some features may not work as expected until restart.
This item belongs to Rhythm Go. Switch to Streaming Mode now to stream and play it?
This item belongs to your Local Library. Switch to Local Mode now to play your offline tracks?
All songs are already in this playlist
No playlists yet\nCreate your first playlist using the + button
